objective: Transform a manually operated multi-component system into a fully reproducible, one-command bootstrap system that initializes infrastructure, backend API, workers, and monitoring stack with correct environment handling and execution flow.
decisions: Adopt root directory (AI-Growth-OS) as single execution point
Replace multi-terminal manual execution with single-command bootstrap.sh
Use Docker Compose inside infra/ for Redis, PostgreSQL, Prometheus, Grafana, n8n
Introduce scripts directory for operational automation
Define script roles: setup.sh (env), run.sh (API), backup.sh (backup), dump_system.sh (debug), bootstrap.sh (full system)
Fix relative path issues using cd $(dirname "$0")/.. pattern
Resolve Python module import error by setting PYTHONPATH to backend directory
Use nohup for background execution of API and worker
Redirect logs to server.log and worker.log
Implement idempotent dependency installation using venv/.deps_installed flag
Avoid repeated pip install execution for performance optimization
Use pkill before starting services to avoid duplicate processes
Treat Prometheus, Grafana, n8n as passive infra services (not manually controlled)
Access observability tools via browser instead of CLI management
Use docker compose down to stop all infra containers
Use tail -f to monitor logs instead of direct terminal output
Separate operator mode (system-level control) from developer mode (manual execution)
inputs: Docker commands (docker compose up/down, docker ps)
outputs: Docker containers running (Redis, PostgreSQL, Prometheus, Grafana, n8n)
notes: System transitioned from fragmented manual execution (infra, backend, worker separately) to unified bootstrap-driven execution. All core components (infra, API, workers, monitoring) are operational through a single command. Remaining limitations include lack of CI/CD, absence of health checks and retry logic in bootstrap, no centralized logging system, and no automated environment variable management. Observability is active via Prometheus polling. System is stable and reproducible but not fully production-hardened.
